title: Implement MeshCore node identity, status, and config read handlers
description: Why this exists: after connecting, a MeshCore client should see a coherent local LICHEN node. What needs to be done: implement supported read handlers for node identity, firmware/app version, radio/local status, device capabilities, battery/power placeholders, position/time placeholders, channel/config readbacks, and adapter limitations. Map values from LICHEN HAL and local status resources where available. Acceptance criteria: client can read supported identity/status/config data without hangs; synthetic or unavailable fields are explicit; unsupported writes/admin operations are rejected deterministically; native_sim tests validate encoded responses.
notes: Implemented no-hardware MeshCore identity/status/config read-handler slice. Adapter now preserves the no-provider baseline SELF_INFO/DEVICE_INFO placeholders when CONFIG_LICHEN_APP_IDENTITY is disabled. When app_identity is enabled, APP_START still returns explicit degraded read-only SELF_INFO with a zero public-key field until a self public key is published; after publication it copies the LICHEN app identity public key byte-for-byte into SELF_INFO[4..35] and uses bounded display/firmware strings in SELF_INFO/DEVICE_INFO without exposing public key through DEVICE_INFO. Added prj_identity.conf and a second native_sim/Twister scenario for identity-enabled coverage while keeping canonical generated vectors in the baseline scenario. Review evidence: Curie confirmed MeshCore SELF_INFO public key offset 4..35/name 58/radio fields and DEVICE_INFO string offsets. Tesla recommended identity-enabled native_sim tests and explicit no-provider handling. Review found production gateway self-identity publication missing; to avoid product-mode regression, MeshCore BLE Kconfig was not changed to require app_identity yet, and follow-up project-LICHEN-9c4y.19 tracks production key publication/gateway-path coverage. Validation on EC2 i-05e27e074db786e22 using /mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN-9c4y6 and ZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES pointing at that worktree: direct baseline command 'west build -p always -b native_sim lichen/tests/meshcore_adapter -d build/meshcore_adapter_baseline -- -DZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=/mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN-9c4y6/lichen && west build -d build/meshcore_adapter_baseline -t run' passed 16, skipped 1 expected; direct identity command with -DCONF_FILE='prj.conf;prj_identity.conf' passed 17, skipped 2 expected; Twister command '/mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN/zephyr/scripts/twister -T lichen/tests/meshcore_adapter -p native_sim --outdir twister-out-meshcore-identity --inline-logs --integration --extra-args ZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=/mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN-9c4y6/lichen' passed 2/2 configurations, 33 executed cases, 3 expected skips, 0 warnings in 23.59s. ELF sizes: baseline text=52351 data=493 bss=8666; identity text=58170 data=561 bss=8924. Earlier local Twister attempt failed before compile due missing local pykwalify; EC2 Twister is authoritative. Generated twister-out directories were removed and are not committed.
close_reason: Completed: optional MeshCore app_identity projection implemented and tested; production gateway self-identity publication deferred to project-LICHEN-9c4y.19.
